task: Resolve global MCP connection timeouts during engine startup

key_learnings: When MCP servers are started using 'npx -y' or 'uvx' in environments with varying network speeds, the initial package download and package update check can take up to 75+ seconds.
The default JSON-RPC request timeout for the `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` Client is 60 seconds (DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MSEC = 60000).
If the child process is blocked downloading/compiling packages during startup, it cannot respond to the initialization handshake, triggering an automatic 'MCP error -32001: Request timed out' error.

Passing an explicit `{ timeout: 180000 }` (3 minutes) option to `client.connect(transport, options)` overrides the default SDK timeout, cleanly allowing slow-booting servers to complete download and launch cycles successfully.
outcomes.patterns_observed: Setting the Promise.race timeout in MidpointX to match the client handshake timeout (180s) ensures total synchronization and prevents premature socket timeouts on Windows.
